repos.brief:
**What it does:** A public GitHub repository that systematically extracts and publishes the hidden system prompts governing major AI assistants—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Copilot, and others—including full tool definitions, skill configurations, and model-specific instruction sets.

**Why now:** The repo gained 8,000+ stars this week and was featured in The Washington Post, coinciding with fresh extractions from GPT-5.6, Claude Sonnet 5, and Claude Design's 48-tool prompt—making it the most current public reference for how frontier models are actually instructed.

**Build with it:** Use the extracted prompts as blueprints to reverse-engineer guardrail patterns, benchmark your own agent's system prompt against production-grade examples, or build a diff-monitoring tool that alerts you when major AI providers quietly update their model behavior.

**What it does:** UI UX Pro Max is a prompt-based AI skill that injects structured design reasoning into coding assistants like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot, giving them 161 rules and 67 UI styles to generate production-quality interfaces instead of generic layouts.

**Why now:** Vibe coding has hit a plateau where AI writes functional code but ships ugly, inconsistent UIs — this week's 4K new stars signal developers are actively hunting for the missing design layer in their AI workflows.

**Build with it:** Drop the skill into your coding assistant and build a polished multi-section landing page, mobile UI, or dashboard in one session, with the AI selecting the right visual pattern, spacing system, and component hierarchy for your specific product type.

**What it does:** Ponytail is a JavaScript plugin for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) that enforces YAGNI-style minimal code generation — making agents prefer a native `<input type="date">` over installing a full datepicker library, averaging 54% fewer lines of code written per task.

**Why now:** It hit 80,000+ GitHub stars this week with 7,913 new stars added, signaling strong community validation of the "less AI-generated bloat" thesis at a moment when agentic coding workflows are becoming standard practice.

**Build with it:** Drop the skill file into your Claude Code or Cursor setup to immediately cut token costs ~20% and session time ~27% on any greenfield or refactor project — particularly valuable if you're building on a token budget or shipping fast on a codebase you'll have to maintain long-term.

**What it does:** Taste-Skill is a collection of portable agent skills (markdown files) that inject design constraints—layout, typography, spacing, motion—into AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor to prevent them from producing generic-looking UIs.

**Why now:** It gained 6,607 stars this week, signaling that AI-generated frontend slop is a recognized problem and developers are actively looking for guardrails as vibe-coding workflows mature.

**Build with it:** Run `npx skills add https://github.com/Leonxlnx/taste-skill` to wire the design skill into your Cursor or Claude Code project, then use the bundled image-generation skills to produce reference boards that the agent uses as visual targets before writing a single line of component code.

**What it does:** CC Switch is a Tauri-built desktop app that manages API keys, providers, and configuration across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, and other AI coding agents from a single interface, with WSL and MCP support baked in.

**Why now:** It gained 3,136 stars this week, signaling a surge of developers frustrated with juggling separate configs across multiple AI coding tools as the ecosystem fragments further.

**Build with it:** Drop CC Switch into your team's dev environment as a standardized provider-switching layer, then build atop its MCP integration to route different coding tasks to different models based on cost or capability.

**What it does:** ECC is an agent harness operating system that layers skills, memory, security scanning, and research-first workflows on top of Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and other AI coding agents—giving them persistent context and operator-level control across sessions.

**Why now:** With 2,889 stars added this week alone, ECC is hitting an inflection point as multi-agent and cross-harness workflows become a practical engineering reality rather than a demo.

**Build with it:** Drop ECC into your existing Claude Code or Cursor setup to give your agents persistent memory and security guardrails, then extend it to orchestrate multi-harness pipelines where one agent researches, another codes, and a third reviews—without starting from scratch each session.

**What it does:** Firecrawl is an API that scrapes, searches, and crawls websites at scale, converting raw web content into clean Markdown, structured JSON, or screenshots that LLMs and agents can consume directly.

**Why now:** With 149K stars and 5,600+ added this week, momentum is peaking alongside growing demand for reliable web context in agentic pipelines where hallucination from stale training data is a real cost.

**Build with it:** Wire Firecrawl into an AI research agent that takes a user query, searches the live web, scrapes the top results, and returns structured JSON summaries—replacing brittle custom scrapers with a single authenticated API call.

**What it does:** Caveman is a prompt skill/plugin that instructs AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and 30+ others) to strip filler from their responses and answer in terse, caveman-style prose while keeping all code, commands, and error output byte-perfect.

**Why now:** The repo jumped 5,458 stars this week, signaling that output token costs and latency are live pain points for developers running agents in tight loops or on metered API plans.

**Build with it:** Drop this into a cost-tracking wrapper that logs token counts before and after enabling the skill, then expose the savings as a dashboard metric to help teams quantify and justify their AI tooling spend.

**What it does:** Agent Reach is a Python CLI and MCP toolkit that gives AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) authenticated, parsed access to Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu, and more — without paying for any platform APIs.

**Why now:** It hit #1 on GitHub Trending this week with 54k+ stars and 5,114 new stars in seven days, and it just shipped a live fix replacing yt-dlp with bili-cli after Bilibili's bot detection broke the previous scraper — showing the maintenance model works in practice.

**Build with it:** Drop it into any agent workflow to let your LLM autonomously research products across social platforms, summarize competitor Reddit threads, or monitor RSS and stock feeds — then pipe the clean text output directly into your prompt chain without writing a single scraper yourself.

**What it does:** OmniRoute is an open-source TypeScript gateway that exposes a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint routing across 237 AI providers (90+ free tiers), with automatic fallback, RTK+Caveman token compression, and native support for tools like Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, and Copilot.

**Why now:** The repo gained nearly 5,000 stars this week, signaling strong community traction, and the aggregated ~1.6B free tokens/month makes it immediately practical for builders who are hitting rate limits or cost walls across fragmented provider accounts.

**Build with it:** Point your existing OpenAI SDK calls at OmniRoute's local endpoint, configure a fallback chain across Gemini, Claude, and DeepSeek free tiers, and ship a coding assistant or agent pipeline that survives individual provider outages and rate limits at zero API cost.
